title: Competition-Pressure Features From Same-Lap Field Context
summary: Recent pit-strategy literature argues competition materially changes optimal stop timing, and official F1 analysis highlights undercut/overcut dependence on rival proximity and tyre warm-up context.
rationale: Recent pit-strategy literature argues competition materially changes optimal stop timing, and official F1 analysis highlights undercut/overcut dependence on rival proximity and tyre warm-up context. Your current best set has some global medians, but not richer same-lap competitive pressure signals.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x LapNumber`, compute leakage-safe relative features such as tyre-life percentile, degradation percentile, lap-delta percentile, position-band medians, and local pack summaries for cars within +/-2 positions. Derive `undercut_pressure_proxy`, `overcut_viability_proxy`, and `pack_pit_pressure` from these cross-sectional ranks rather than fixed thresholds.
expected_effect: Can improve ranking among borderline stop candidates on the same lap, where absolute tyre age is less informative than relative strategic vulnerability.
risk: Cross-sectional features may be noisy in thin groups or weird sessions like testing; use shrinkage toward race-level medians.
